title: Yang Zhilin scholarly reference tree: HotpotQA to Kimi
caption: Conceptual gitGraph-style scholarly journey, not git history. Citation edges are curated rather than exhaustive; the explicit citation_edges array is authoritative because Mermaid gitGraph cannot represent arbitrary citation DAGs.
scope: One-hop, high-signal ancestors and descendants around the five user-supplied seed papers, plus a small post-PhD bridge. Recent Kimi forward citations require periodic refresh.

branches.label: Selected ancestors
branches.summary: Curated, high-signal works cited by or conceptually foundational to Yang's seed papers; not an exhaustive bibliography.

nodes.title: Attention Is All You Need (2017)
nodes.detail: Ancestor: self-attention and the Transformer architecture; selected because Transformer-XL and XLNet explicitly extend the Transformer family.

nodes.title: Self-Attention with Relative Position Representations (2018)
nodes.detail: Ancestor: relative-position attention is a direct conceptual precursor to Transformer-XL's relative positional formulation.

nodes.title: BERT: Pre-training of Deep Bidirectional Transformers (2018)
nodes.detail: Ancestor/contrast: XLNet explicitly frames permutation language modeling against BERT-style masked denoising.

nodes.title: SQuAD: 100,000+ Questions for Machine Comprehension (2016)
nodes.detail: Ancestor: major extractive QA dataset used by HotpotQA as context for the limitations of predominantly single-document QA.

nodes.title: Constructing Datasets for Multi-hop Reading Comprehension (2018)
nodes.detail: Ancestor: WikiHop/QAngaroo is a key earlier multi-hop QA benchmark contrasted with HotpotQA's natural questions and supporting facts.

branches.label: CMU PhD core
branches.summary: Yang's three best-known CMU-era contributions in multi-hop QA, long-context modeling, and generalized autoregressive pretraining.

nodes.title: HotpotQA: Diverse, Explainable Multi-hop QA (2018)
nodes.detail: Yang-led PhD-era dataset: 113k Wikipedia-based QA pairs, multi-document reasoning, sentence-level supporting facts, and comparison questions. DOI 10.18653/v1/D18-1259.

nodes.title: Transformer-XL: Beyond a Fixed-Length Context (2019)
nodes.detail: Yang co-led this long-context language-model architecture, adding segment recurrence and relative positional encoding. ACL Anthology ID P19-1285; DOI 10.18653/v1/P19-1285.

nodes.title: XLNet: Generalized Autoregressive Pretraining (2019)
nodes.detail: Yang co-led XLNet, combining permutation-based autoregressive pretraining with ideas from Transformer-XL; NeurIPS 2019.

branches.label: Selected scholarly children
branches.summary: Representative papers that cite or directly build on the PhD-era seed papers.

nodes.title: Dynamically Fused Graph Network for Multi-hop Reasoning (2019)
nodes.detail: Selected child: a prominent HotpotQA-era graph-reasoning system that cites/uses HotpotQA.

nodes.title: A Simple Yet Strong Pipeline for HotpotQA (2020)
nodes.detail: Selected child: demonstrates that a comparatively simple BERT pipeline can be highly competitive on HotpotQA.

nodes.title: Compressive Transformers for Long-Range Sequence Modelling (2020)
nodes.detail: Selected child: extends the recurrent memory line associated with Transformer-XL by compressing older memories.

nodes.title: Longformer: The Long-Document Transformer (2020)
nodes.detail: Selected child: long-context Transformer work that situates itself among approaches including Transformer-XL.

nodes.title: ELECTRA: Pre-training Text Encoders as Discriminators (2020)
nodes.detail: Selected child: influential pretraining work that compares with and cites XLNet.

branches.label: Post-PhD research bridge
branches.summary: Selected Yang coauthored work linking the PhD program to later foundation-model research.

nodes.title: GLM: Autoregressive Blank Infilling (2022)
nodes.detail: Yang coauthored GLM, continuing generalized autoregressive pretraining through blank infilling.

nodes.title: P-Tuning v2: Prompt Tuning Across Scales and Tasks (2022)
nodes.detail: Yang coauthored this parameter-efficient adaptation work, part of the post-PhD path toward large-model systems.

branches.label: Kimi technical ancestors
branches.summary: Selected technical ancestors for RL post-training, multimodality, and sparse experts; exact report-level references should be checked in the PDFs.

nodes.title: Proximal Policy Optimization Algorithms (2017)
nodes.detail: Selected RL ancestor for policy optimization in language-model post-training.

nodes.title: Training Language Models to Follow Instructions with Human Feedback (2022)
nodes.detail: Selected LLM post-training ancestor: instruction tuning plus reinforcement learning from human feedback.

nodes.title: An Image Is Worth 16x16 Words: Vision Transformer (2021)
nodes.detail: Selected vision ancestor for transformer-based image encoding.

nodes.title: Switch Transformers: Scaling to Trillion Parameter Models (2021)
nodes.detail: Selected sparse Mixture-of-Experts ancestor relevant to Kimi-VL's efficient activated-parameter design.

nodes.title: Kimi k1.5: Scaling RL with LLMs (2025)
nodes.detail: Kimi Team report, with Zhilin Yang among the authors. It emphasizes 128K-context RL, improved policy optimization, multimodal reasoning, and long-to-short transfer. arXiv:2501.12599.

branches.label: Kimi multimodal
branches.summary: Kimi's 2025 efficient vision-language and multimodal-reasoning report.

nodes.title: Kimi-VL Technical Report (2025)
nodes.detail: Kimi Team report, with Zhilin Yang among the authors. It describes an MoE VLM with 2.8B activated decoder parameters, native-resolution MoonViT, 128K context, agent capabilities, and a thinking variant. arXiv:2504.07491.

branches.label: Living frontier
branches.summary: Recent forward-citation data is a living, database-dependent frontier and remains open for refresh.

nodes.title: OPEN: Refresh 2025-2026 citing-paper frontier
nodes.detail: Forward citations for recent Kimi reports change quickly. Re-query OpenAlex, Semantic Scholar, Google Scholar, and Crossref/OpenCitations before publication; verify each edge in the citing paper's reference list.

duplicate_bibtex_resolution.problem: Both Kimi reports were exported with team2025kimi; BibTeX keys are local labels and must be unique within a bibliography.

sources.purpose: Dataset lineage and downstream benchmark context

limitations: A gitGraph is a visual metaphor for a citation network; it cannot encode arbitrary many-to-many citation edges.
Ancestor/child selection is curated for readability, not exhaustive.
Database citation counts and recent citing-paper lists can differ because Google Scholar, Semantic Scholar, OpenAlex, Crossref, and OpenCitations have different coverage.
